page: Microsoft Azure E16bs_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EBSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 48 MB of L3 cache and advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration like AES and SHA_NI. The instance is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, relying instead on remote premium block storage.

Benchmark data indicates solid mid-range performance across standard compute and memory tasks. The single-core stress-ng score is 1899.99, while the multi-core score reaches 15534.08. Memory bandwidth is measured at 834.28 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 100.1 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. In database and memory-intensive operations, the server achieves a Passmark database score of 7985.51 and handles 15.81 million Redis SET operations per second. For lightweight language model tasks, it demonstrates strong text generation performance of 268.04 tokens per second using a 135M parameter model, though performance scales down to 11.94 tokens per second with a larger 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E16bs_v5 offers a balanced resource density for memory-heavy workloads without the added expense of local NVMe drives or dedicated GPU hardware. The absence of local storage means storage costs are decoupled, allowing users to scale remote block storage independently. This profile is highly suitable for relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, software compilation, and medium-scale web serving applications that require substantial memory capacity relative to compute resources.
description: Microsoft Azure E16bs_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the EBSv5 family, powered by a dedicated 16-vCPU Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high 8.0 GB memory-per-core ratio, while omitting local storage and GPU hardware. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the server delivers stable performance, highlighted by a multi-core stress-ng score of 15534.08 and a Redis throughput of 15.81 million SET operations per second. It also demonstrates strong efficiency in lightweight LLM text generation, reaching 268.04 tokens per second on a 135M model. Lacking local disks, it relies on remote premium block storage, making it a cost-effective choice for memory-intensive workloads. This configuration is well-suited for database operations, in-memory caching, and software compilation tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16bs_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for high-performance block storage workloads without local disks.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16bs_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, built for remote block storage workloads.
tagline: An Intel-powered virtual machine offering high memory density and optimized remote block storage performance for enterprise database workloads.

High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core
Optimized for premium remote block storage with no local disks
Delivers 15.81 million Redis SET operations per second
Suited for database hosting, in-memory caching, and software compilation
